data.title: Brazil's Gol to launch long-haul routes from Rio with new A330s
data.description:
By Gabriel Araujo and Luciana Magalhaes

Brazilian airline Gol is set to turn Rio de Janeiro's Galeao international airport into its hub for the new Airbus A330-900 aircraft entering the fleet, Gol Chief Executive announced on Friday.

Until now, Gol has operated exclusively with Boeing 737 aircraft.

The new aircraft addition will be followed by the implementation of a new direct route between Rio and New York starting in July, Gol's CEO Celso Ferrer said at an event in Galeao alongside Brazil's President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva.

"The union between two key tourism and business hubs in South and North America opens up a range of options and opportunities for customers across the continent," the firm said in a statement released later.

The airline is initially receiving five A330-900s, which are wide-body aircraft with approximately 300 seats capable of flying routes of up to 15 hours.

The planes will be gradually incorporated into the company's fleet between 2026 and 2027.

Reuters reported earlier in the day, citing sources, that Gol would announce Galeao airport as the hub for its new Airbus A330-900.

Gol is also set to offer direct flights from Rio to Paris and Lisbon starting late this year, Ferrer said.

One of the sources said earlier Gol has sought slots in several major European cities, including Porto and London, though not all will necessarily become destinations.

data.title: ArcBest sees Q1 tonnage, shipments up; expects up to $2M asset-light operating income
data.description:
ArcBest provided a Q1 2026 update showing higher tonnage and shipments, mixed revenue metrics, and asset-light segment expecting up to $2 million non-GAAP operating income.

Key Highlights:

  • Asset-based: QTD billed revenue/day up 1%, tonnage/day up 6%, shipments/day up 2% versus year-ago period.
  • Revenue mix: Billed revenue per CWT down ~5% QTD due to freight-profile shifts and lower fuel surcharge.
  • Sequential seasonality: First-quarter operating ratio expected to rise ~100–200 bps sequentially (better than typical ~260 bps increase).
  • Asset-light: QTD revenue/day up 6%, shipments/day up 13%, expects up to $2M non-GAAP operating income (excludes ~$3M purchase-accounting amortization).

Original SEC Filing: ARCBEST CORP /DE/ [ ARCB ] - 8-K - Mar. 06, 2026

data.title: US FDA approves Lantheus' new prostate cancer imaging formulation
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on Friday approved a new formulation of Lantheus Holdings' prostate cancer imaging agent, aimed at improving scanning access through increased production capacity.

Shares of the company were up about 3% in extended trading.

The imaging agent, Pylarify, targets prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), a protein that is commonly elevated in prostate cancer cells and used to detect metastatic or recurrent disease in patients.

The new formulation is designed to optimize the manufacturing process and is expected to increase the batch size by about 50%, while also improving margins for the company.

data.title: Key facts: Petroleo Brasileiro reports $18.1B net income; $1.54B payout planned
data.description:

  • Petroleo Brasileiro SA (PETR3) will pay 8.1 billion reais ($1.54 billion) in interest on equity, about 0.63 real per share, in two installments in May and June.2
  • CEO Magda Chambriard announced that Petrobras will not transfer sudden oil price volatility to consumers, amid recent disruptions in global energy flows due to conflicts in Iran.3

data.title: U.S. Stocks Extend Slide On Oil Gains, Job Losses — Market Talk
data.description:
1605 ET - Stocks fall after the U.S. reports a surprise 92,000 decline in February payrolls, while oil prices spike above $90 a barrel, the biggest weekly gain on record. Investors worry that the higher oil could drive other prices higher as the conflict between Iran and the U.S. drags on into its seventh day, with no let up in sight. The twin worries about higher inflation along with a weakened labor market complicates the picture for Fed policy makers. DJIA falls 453 points, or 1%, to 47501, the S&P 500 loses 1.3% to 6740 and the Nasdaq drops 1.6% to 22387. The Dow leads the weekly decliners, falling 3%. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

1559 ET - Okta is positioned to benefit from the growing adoption of artificial-intelligence agents, BMO analyst Keith Bachman writes in a note, upgrading the stock to outperform. Organizations using agents need a unified, identity-first security model, Bachman writes, citing reports from Gartner. Smaller, newer vendors lack the scale, context, trust, and integrations that Okta has as an established player, he adds. "We think that increasing rates of agent use and agent abuse can help drive more comprehensive identity investments among enterprises, as well as agent specific identity management purchases," he writes. "We believe that Okta can benefit from both trends over the next several years." Shares gain 1.4%. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

1549 ET - Treasury yields and the dollar fall at the end of a volatile week as U.S. reports a surprising 92,000 job loss in February, while markets fret about inflation driven by the war in Iran. February CPI data is due next week, ahead of the Fed's March 18 meeting. The WSJ Dollar Index slips 0.1%, still on track for its best week since October. The 10-year yield adds 0.170 percentage point this week, to 4.131%, despite a 0.014 p.p. decline today. The two-year rises 0.177 p.p. for the week, to 3.554%, and slips 0.045 p.p. today. Both have their largest weekly rise since April. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1544 ET - Domino's Pizza is likely to see a summer tailwind from watch parties for the FIFA World Cup, Deutsche Bank analyst Lauren Silberman writes in a note. She notes that Domino's sold about 40% more pizzas on Super Bowl Sunday, which drew about 125 million U.S. viewers, than on a typical Sunday. About 25 million U.S. viewers tuned in to the 2022 World Cup Final, and 19.9 million watched the U.S.-England match. "Given the increase in the overall number of games that will be played, the fact that the games will be played in U.S. time zones, and that soccer has been increasing in popularity over the years, we believe the impact on US sales could be more meaningful than seen historically," Silberman writes. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

1543 ET - Domino's Pizza is well-positioned to gain market share despite an overall negative sentiment around the U.S. pizza industry from the growth of delivery aggregators and GLP-1s, Deutsche Bank analyst Lauren Silberman writes in a note. The 2025 combined 61% market share of the four top pizza players — Domino's, Pizza Hut, Papa Johns, and Little Caesars — is in line with 2019, Silberman notes, suggesting that the companies are taking share from each other rather than independent operators. Domino's has outperformed Pizza Hut and Papa Johns over the past two years, and its guidance implies system sales growth of about 5%, with promotions, menu innovations, and the FIFA World Cup providing near-term catalysts. Domino's is up 1.5%. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

1444 ET - Gap shares should see upside ahead with multiple drivers despite 4Q results "that were a hair light," but in-line fiscal-year guidance, according to Baird in a research note. Analysts Mark Altschwager and Katie McGuire expect the apparel company to get a boost from strong consumer spending due to tax refunds, lower tariffs and improvements from its investments in marketing, stores and technology. With Gap sliding 14% post 4Q results, the analysts say "we're buyers," noting they see little downside given reasonable guidance and a good start to the current quarter. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1431 ET - Samsara's guidance seems conservative given the safety software company's recent across-the-board beat, according to JPMorgan in a research note. The analysts say Samsara ended the year with annual recurring revenue of $1.9 billion, or 30% year-over-year growth. Net new 4Q ARR saw a bump of 33% to $145 million, the highest net new growth in the past eight quarters. On the analyst call, Samsara's management emphasized strong contributions across major sectors, as well as continued European momentum, the analysts say. Samsara's outlook of low 20% ARR growth for fiscal 2027 "appears conservative given accelerating momentum," according to the analysts. Samsara surges 18%.(amira.mckee@wsj.com)

1410 ET - Despite this week's sharp rise in crude prices, the market appears to be "unusually circumspect" about current and coming supply losses related to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, Vikas Dwivedi of Macquarie Group says in a note. "The timeline for an extremely large oil price move is very short," and "a few weeks of Hormuz closure will create a domino effect of events that could push crude to $150 or higher." He notes production shut-ins in Iraq and Kuwait, and Qatar's suspension of LNG production. Greater production shut-ins could emerge through the next week if the disruption persists, depending on relative storage positions, Dwivedi says. "In our view, the final cuts would occur in Saudi Arabia roughly 20 days from now." (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1357 ET - The Trump administration has collected approximately $166 billion in payments for tariffs made under Ieepa, U.S. Customs and Border Protection says in a court filing. The Supreme Court ruled last month that Ieepa did not authorize President Trump to impose sweeping global tariffs, and the Court of International Trade ordered the administration to begin refunding payments in a ruling this week. CBP says it is developing a streamlined tariff refund system that it hopes will be operational in 45 days. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

1348 ET - It would take upward of 4.4 million man-hours for U.S. Customs and Border Protection to refund tariff payments made under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act using existing processes, the agency says in a court filing, adding that it cannot comply with a court ruling ordering the Trump administration to begin the process of reimbursing importers. The agency says it is developing a streamlined system to process tariff refunds, which it estimated will save more than 4 million hours compared to existing manual procedures. CBP says it is "making all possible efforts" to have the system operational in 45 days. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

1344 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. rose by four this week to 411, and was down by 75 from a year ago, oil services company Baker Hughes reports. Efficiency gains, industry consolidation and low oil prices have kept U.S. producers from drilling more, and the recent spike in prices over the Persian Gulf conflict is seen unlikely to spur activity in the near term. "U.S. producers are much more disciplined, and they need to see a sustained higher price," says Andrejka Bernatova, CEO of energy-focused special purpose acquisition company Dynamix Corporation. Oil prices were already close to breakeven for the Permian basin before the conflict. "So I think producers in the U.S. have to wait. Is this really sustainable? I'm not going to react to a 10-day oil blip," Bernatova adds.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1334 ET - Gap slides after missing Wall Street's 4Q EPS estimates and reporting weaker sales for its struggling Athleta brand. But Gap's entry into beauty should boost both its top and bottom lines, Jefferies analysts say in a note. The company is seeing encouraging early results from its beauty pilot at Old Navy, with management noting higher engagement and bigger purchases, the analysts say. The company also plans to reintroduce fragrances in 2026, according to the analysts. However, while the beauty offerings should help results over time, the analysts say they expect the Athleta headwinds to continue. Gap drops 13%.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: First Banc 10-K: Net income $34.4M; Diluted EPS $3.07 on NII expansion
data.description:
First Banc reported net income of $34.4 million, up 27.2% year‑over‑year, driven by expanded net interest income and margin improvement; diluted earnings per share rose to $3.07, an increase of $0.64. Total interest income increased to $160.3 million (tax‑equivalent up $11.5 million) supporting net interest income of $77.4 million (tax‑equivalent $80.2 million) and a net interest margin that widened to 2.63% from 2.29%. Non‑interest income and operating expenses moved modestly higher, with wealth management contributing to fee growth while staffing and other operating costs increased.

Financial Highlights

  • Total interest income: $160.3 million, up 7.7% year‑over‑year (tax‑equivalent interest up $11.5M)
  • Net interest income: $77.4 million (tax‑equivalent $80.2 million); net interest margin expanded to 2.63% from 2.29%
  • Non‑interest income: $17.3 million, up $1.0 million (6.0%)
  • Total non‑interest expense (operating expense proxy): $50.9 million, up $3.8 million (8.0%)
  • Net income: $34.4 million, up $7.3 million (27.2%)
  • Diluted earnings per share: $3.07, up $0.64 (26.3%)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Net income growth was driven primarily by expansion in net interest income and an improved margin.
  • Deposit and funding mix: Core local deposits increased by $77 million as lower‑cost local deposits replaced higher‑cost wholesale certificates of deposit.
  • Lending and portfolio strategy: Loan balances grew by $53 million, reflecting a shift from investment securities toward higher‑yielding loan originations and targeted lending activity.
  • Wealth management: Wealth management assets under management reached $1.384 billion, contributing to the increase in non‑interest income through fee revenue.
  • Operations and staffing: Efficiency ratio improved to 52.09% despite higher staff-related costs driven by salary and retirement actions; operating expenses increased, reflecting investment in personnel and other operating areas.

Original SEC Filing: First Bancorp, Inc /ME/ [ FNLC ] - 10-K - Mar. 06, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Bluejay 10-K: Net loss $6.85M, operating loss $6.95M; cash through Q3 2026
data.description:
Bluejay reported an operating loss of $6.95 million and a net loss of $6.85 million for the year, an improvement versus the prior year driven in part by an other income adjustment; basic net loss applicable to common shareholders was $6.85 million. The company ended the period with $5.16 million in cash and cash equivalents and estimates that current resources will fund operations into the third quarter of 2026, after which additional capital will be required.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating loss: $(6.95) million, a decrease versus prior year operating loss of $(7.17) million.
  • Net loss: $(6.85) million, improved from $(7.72) million in the prior year; improvement includes other income adjustment.
  • Net loss applicable to common shareholders: $(6.85) million, compared with $(20.94) million in the prior year (prior year included a $13.22 million deemed dividend).
  • Cash and cash equivalents: $5.16 million, with management estimating sufficiency through Q3 2026 absent additional financing.

Business Highlights

  • Product development: Advanced the Symphony rapid blood diagnostics platform targeting IL-6, achieving completed development milestones and continuing its clinical program.
  • Clinical focus: Prioritizing ICU/ER sepsis application with a planned FDA submission pathway and ongoing clinical trials intended to support regulatory clearance.
  • Manufacturing scale-up: Building manufacturing capabilities via contract manufacturing organizations and reduced technology transfer spending projected for 2025.
  • Go-to-market posture: Sales and marketing expenditures were cut sharply in 2025 to conserve cash while preparing for regulatory submission and commercialization readiness.
  • Liquidity-driven operations: Operational plans and timing are constrained by capital needs; the company’s fundraising strategy may affect the pacing of clinical trials and commercialization activities.

Original SEC Filing: Bluejay Diagnostics, Inc. [ BJDX ] - 10-K - Mar. 06, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Black Spade Acquisition III Co 10-K: Net loss $(86,377), Cash $3.279M, Minimal operating cash flow
data.description:
Black Spade Acquisition III Co reported a net loss of $(86,377) for the period Aug 21–Dec 31, 2025, driven by general and administrative expenses and with no operating revenues. Cash held outside the Trust Account was $3.279258 million as of Dec 31, 2025, and net cash provided by operating activities was $4,258 for the period. The company is a newly formed blank-check vehicle focused on fintech and digitized financial infrastructure and completed its IPO on Jan 7, 2026 to finance a future business combination.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $(86,377) — reported net loss for the period Aug 21–Dec 31, 2025, primarily reflecting general and administrative costs.
  • Cash: $3.279258 million — cash held outside the Trust Account as of Dec 31, 2025.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities: $4,258 — minimal operating cash flow for the period.

Business Highlights

  • Business model & focus: Formed as a blank-check company targeting fintech and digitized financial infrastructure businesses for a single business combination.
  • Operational status: No operating revenues to date; activities through Dec 31, 2025 were limited to company formation, IPO preparation and target identification.
  • Capital formation milestone: Completed initial public offering on Jan 7, 2026, generating trust funds intended to finance a business combination and producing outside-trust cash to fund diligence and transaction evaluation.
  • Post-IPO operations: Anticipates earning non-operating interest income on the Trust Account; outside-trust funds will be used for target evaluation, due diligence and execution of the transaction.

Original SEC Filing: Black Spade Acquisition III Co [ BIII ] - 10-K - Mar. 06, 2026

data.title: Aware 2025 10-K: $17.3M Revenue, $(0.28) EPS
data.description:
Aware reported revenue of $17.3 million for the year, essentially flat with $17.4 million in 2024, while posting a net loss of $5.9 million, or $(0.28) per share, driven by higher R&D, cost of revenue and general and administrative expenses. The operating loss widened to $(6.6) million from $(5.5) million a year earlier. Interest and other income declined to $0.9 million as cash balances and rates fell.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $17.3 million (flat vs. $17.4 million in 2024)
  • Operating loss: $(6.6) million (increased from $(5.5) million in 2024)
  • Net loss: $(5.9) million (vs. $(4.4) million in 2024); includes $0.2 million write-off of note receivable
  • Net loss per share, basic and diluted: $(0.28) (vs. $(0.21) in 2024)
  • Interest and other income: $0.9 million (down from $1.2 million)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and trend: Revenue remained stable year-over-year with a modest shift from license sales toward software maintenance as the largest component (51%).
  • Sales channels: Continued multi-channel distribution through integrators, OEMs, VARs and direct sales.
  • SaaS growth: Subscription/SaaS revenue increased from $0.1 million to $0.4 million, indicating early expansion of cloud-based offerings.
  • R&D investment: Engineering headcount rose to 45, contributing to higher engineering costs and supporting a new product roadmap aimed at broader biometrics solutions; R&D spending is expected to remain higher into 2026.
  • Cost dynamics: Cost of revenue increased partly due to higher license costs.
  • Customer retention and recurring revenue: Strong maintenance renewals underpin recurring revenue, with subscription arrangements expanding modestly.

Original SEC Filing: AWARE INC /MA/ [ AWRE ] - 10-K - Mar. 06, 2026

data.title: ArcBest Files 8K - Regulation FD >ARCB
data.description:
ArcBest Corp. (ARCB) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 06, 2026.

ArcBest (R) is providing an update on the most recent information related to its first quarter 2026 financial results and business trends.

Summary Operating and Financial Impacts

● Statistics for February 2026 are preliminary but are not expected to differ materially from actual results.

● There were 20.5 workdays in January 2026 and 22.0 workdays in January 2025.

● There were 20.0 workdays in February 2026 and 20.0 workdays in February 2025.

● The first quarter to date reflects the period from January 1 through February 28, 2026, compared to the same period in 2025.

Year-over-Year Business Trends

1) Revenue for undelivered freight is deferred for financial statement purposes in accordance with the Asset-Based segment revenue recognition policy. Billed revenue per day has not been adjusted for the portion of revenue deferred for financial statement purposes.

In February, tonnage growth was driven by an increase in weight per shipment, primarily due to changes in freight profile. Revenue per shipment was impacted by lower revenue per hundredweight, offset by the higher weight per shipment. Revenue per hundredweight decreased due to changes in freight profile and lower fuel surcharge revenue.

Sequentially, from January to February, both shipments and tonnage per day increased 1%, while weight per shipment was down less than one percent. Revenue per shipment increased 2%, due to a 3% increase in revenue per hundredweight, offset in part by the slightly lower weight per shipment. Revenue per hundredweight improved due to pricing gains and higher fuel surcharge revenue.

Historically, ABF's non-GAAP operating ratio increases by approximately 260 basis points from the fourth quarter to the first quarter. We currently expect our first quarter operating ratio to increase approximately 100 to 200 basis points sequentially, an improvement relative to typical seasonality, due in part to a softer-than-normal fourth quarter, while still reflecting ongoing softness across the industry.

Asset-Light Operating Segment

In February, Asset-Light daily revenue increased year-over-year. Shipment growth was led by Managed; however, its smaller average shipment size resulted in a lower overall revenue per shipment.

On a sequential basis, from January to February, daily revenue increased 10% while shipments increased 7% and revenue per shipment increased 3%, due to the continued strength in Managed.

For the first quarter, we currently expect non-GAAP operating income of up to $2 million. This estimate excludes GAAP impacts from purchase accounting amortization, which we anticipate will total approximately $3 million for the quarter. We remain committed to maintaining yield discipline, managing costs, and positioning the segment for sustainable, long-term profitability.

The following is a "safe harbor" statement under the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995: Certain statements and information in this report may constitute "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, including, among others, statements regarding (i) our expectations about our intrinsic value or our prospects for growth and value creation and (ii) our financial outlook, position, strategies, goals, and expectations. Terms such as "anticipate," "believe," "could," "designed," "estimate," "expect," "forecast," "foresee," "intend," "likely," "may," "plan," "predict," "project," "scheduled," "seek," "should," "would," and similar expressions and the negatives of such terms are intended to identify forward-looking statements. These statements are based on management's beliefs, assumptions, and expectations based on currently available information, are not guarantees of future performance, and involve certain risks and uncertainties (some of which are beyond our control). Although we believe that the expectations reflected in these forward-looking statements are reasonable as and when made, we cannot provide assurance that our expectations will prove to be correct and caution the reader not to place undue reliance on our forward-looking statements. Actual outcomes and results could materially differ from what is expressed, implied, or forecasted in these statements due to a number of factors, including, but not limited to: data breaches, cybersecurity incidents, and/or interruptions or failures of our information systems that we depend on, including software programs and applications provided by third parties; untimely or ineffective development and implementation of, or failure to realize the potential benefits associated with, new or enhanced technology or processes; the loss or reduction of business from multiple large customers or an overall reduction in our customer base; the timing and performance of growth initiatives and the ability to manage our cost structure; the cost, integration, and performance of future acquisitions and the inability to realize the anticipated benefits of the acquisition; unsolicited takeover proposals, proxy contests, and other proposals or actions by activist investors; maintaining our corporate reputation and intellectual property rights; failure to achieve market acceptance or generate adequate returns through our Vaux TM technologies; establishing and maintaining adequate internal controls over financial reporting; disruptions in domestic or global manufacturing activity, supply chains, and related changes in spending, resulting in material reductions in freight volumes; competitive initiatives and pricing pressures; increased prices for and decreased availability of equipment, including new revenue equipment, and higher costs of equipment-related operating expenses such as maintenance, fuel, and related taxes; availability of fuel, the effect of volatility in fuel prices and the associated changes in fuel surcharges on securing increases in base freight rates, and the inability to collect fuel surcharges; relationships with employees, including unions, and our ability to attract, retain, and upskill employees; unfavorable terms of, or the inability to reach agreement on, future collective bargaining agreements or a workforce stoppage by our employees covered under ABF Freight's collective bargaining agreement; union employee wages and benefits, including changes in required contributions to multiemployer plans; availability and cost of reliable third-party services; our ability to secure independent owner-operators and/or operational or regulatory issues related to our use of their services; litigation or claims asserted against us; the effects, costs and potential liabilities related to changes in and compliance with, or violation of, existing or future governmental laws and regulations, including, but not limited to, environmental laws and regulations, such as emissions-control regulations and fuel efficiency regulations; default on covenants of financing arrangements and the availability and terms of future financing arrangements; our ability to generate sufficient cash from operations to support significant ongoing capital expenditure requirements and other business initiatives; self-insurance claims, insurance premium costs, and loss of our ability to self-insure; potential impairment of long-lived assets and goodwill and intangible assets; external events which may adversely affect us or the third parties who provide services for us, for which our business continuity plans may not adequately prepare us, including, but not limited to, the occurrence of natural disasters, public health crises, geopolitical conflicts, acts of terrorism or war, cybersecurity incidents, or trade restrictions; general economic conditions and related shifts in market demand that impact the performance and needs of industries we serve and/or limit our customers' access to adequate financial resources; seasonal fluctuations, adverse weather conditions, natural disasters, and climate change; and other financial, operational, and legal risks and uncertainties detailed from time to time in ArcBest Corporation's public filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC").

For additional information regarding known material factors that could cause our actual results to differ from those expressed in these forward-looking statements, please see our filings with the SEC, including our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, and Current Reports on Form 8-K.

Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements, which speak only as of the date hereof. We undertake no obligation to publicly update or revise any forward-looking statements after the date they are made, whether as a result of new information, future events, or otherwise.

data.title: Western Alliance Sues Jefferies Over Soured Loan, Records Charge-Off — WSJ
data.description:
By Gina Heeb and Connor Hart

Shares of Western Alliance dropped double digits Friday after the regional bank sued Jefferies Financial Group over a soured $126.4 million loan tied to bankrupt auto supplier First Brands Group.

The Phoenix-based company alleged breach of contract and fraud and said it would charge off the loan. The lawsuit was filed against Jefferies and its subsidiary Leucadia Asset Management in New York Supreme Court.

Western Alliance Bancorp. said it had been informed by Jefferies that it wouldn't repay the loan, which had been collateralized by accounts receivable purchased from First Brands Group.

Western Alliance shares sank nearly 14%. Jefferies's stock was down more than 9% and on track for its lowest close in more than two years.

"I'm not going to speculate as to what's going on at Jefferies as to why they couldn't make payments," Western Alliance Chief Executive Ken Vecchione said on a call with analysts after the suit was filed. "We were very surprised that they decided to pull back. To me, it's quite shocking."

Wall Street has been on edge about private credit, with several high-profile funds facing withdrawals. The worries have been brewing since last year after troubles at First Brands and other firms set off fears about credit stress that could be lurking in the funds. Jefferies had deep ties to First Brands, the Journal reported. Last month, a British mortgage company became another black eye for private credit.

On the Friday call with analysts, Vecchione said the notice from Jefferies came a week before it was supposed to receive its next payment from Jefferies. He said he met with Jefferies CEO Richard Handler this past Sunday "to emphasize that collaboration was preferable" but that "litigation is the path that Jefferies left us with."

In a statement, Jefferies said it believed the lawsuit "is without merit" and that the involved fund had acted in good faith and with goodwill to the bank.

Other bank stocks fell more than 4% on Friday, more than the broader market.

"This is not a new cockroach," said Casey Haire, a bank analyst at Autonomous Research. "This is one that we thought we had put behind us and it's rearing its ugly head."

Western Alliance said it had taken steps to minimize financial pain from the most recent charge-off, such as the pursuit of $50 million of securities gains and $50 million of incremental expense savings.

Western Alliance was caught up in other credit turmoil last year in a lawsuit by fellow regional bank Zions Bancorp. That suit revealed that Western Alliance had sought to recover roughly $100 million from a set of borrowers that were accused of fraud.

On Friday, Western Alliance pointed to a $25 million fraud insurance policy against that reserve. It said the reserves still appeared to be appropriate and that it would revisit them, as well as any related charge-offs.

Write to Gina Heeb at gina.heeb@wsj.com and Connor Hart at Connor.Hart@wsj.com

data.title: SPAC With Backing From PE Firm Patriot Financial Files for $200M IPO
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

A blank-check company backed by private-equity firm Patriot Financial Partners filed for a $200 million initial public offering.

Jack Kopnisky, a senior partner with Radnor, Penn.-based Patriot Financial Partners and former chief executive of Sterling Bancorp, is CEO of Patriot Acquisition Corp.

Patriot Acquisition Corp. is seeking a Nasdaq Global Market listing. The company expects to pursue deal targets in specialty finance, "particularly specialty lending companies with unique technology platforms who can generate loan volume digitally for commercial and consumer customers in an efficient manner, and fee-based fintech and payment companies." It will also consider community-banking investments.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com
